prompt:
Take the FIRST provided reference image (approved step 17 v4 — hand from upper-left lowering the stubby pill-bottle protector tube into the DH-branded zip-seal specimen bag with QR code) and reproduce it PIXEL-FAITHFULLY in every element — same hand from upper-left, same bag silhouette and proportions, same parted zip seal, same DIALED HEALTH bag-front logo (offset diagonal: DIALED top-LEFT + EKG-spike horizontal line + HEALTH bottom-RIGHT), same square QR code on the centered/lower area of the bag, same line-art style on pure white background, same uniform medium-weight black ink line work.

CRITICAL — RESTORE THE TUBE PROPORTIONS from this v4 reference: short stubby PILL-BOTTLE silhouette, total tube height ≈ 1.5x diameter (NOT taller, NOT wider). Body-to-cap split ≈ 60/40 (white body slightly taller than the black cap). Diameter narrow enough that the hand still dominates the composition — the tube fits comfortably between thumb and fingers with room to spare. The previous regeneration made the capsule far too wide and too short. Restore the v4 chunky-narrow chess-pawn silhouette exactly.

MANDATORY MODIFICATION — the embossed text on the protector tube. Replace any existing text with the following arrangement so that reading TOP-TO-BOTTOM down the side of the assembled upright tube spells 'DIALED HEALTH' letter by letter, linearly:

  • BLACK CAP (top section, pinched by the hand above the zip seal): the word 'DIALED' is embossed running VERTICALLY from the top of the cap downward, with each letter stacked one above the next. First letter D at the very top of the cap, then I, then A, then L, then E, then final D just above the cap/body seam. Each letter is a separate horizontal letter (NOT rotated 90°, NOT lying on its side) — each letter sits upright as normal capital letters, just stacked vertically one below the other to spell DIALED top-down.
  • WHITE BODY (bottom section, lower portion visible faintly through the translucent bag): the word 'HEALTH' is embossed running VERTICALLY from the top of the body downward, with each letter stacked one above the next. First letter H at the top of the body (just below the cap/body seam), then E, A, L, T, final H just above the body's base. Each letter is upright (NOT rotated, NOT sideways) — stacked vertically one below the other to spell HEALTH top-down.
  • Combined top-to-bottom reading of the assembled upright tube: D - I - A - L - E - D - H - E - A - L - T - H = 'DIALED HEALTH' linearly, letter by letter, top-down.

TEXT STYLE — both words must be rendered as OUTLINED LETTERS (line-art only, NOT solid-filled). Each letter is drawn as a clean thin black outline forming the letter shape, with the letter's interior left UNFILLED (transparent — showing the cap's black or the body's white through the letter). Bold sans-serif capital letterforms, but rendered as just the outline. The previous regeneration used solid-filled letters — that is WRONG. Match the line-art aesthetic of the rest of the illustration: outlined letters only, like embossed lettering shown in line drawing.
  • For DIALED on the BLACK cap: thin WHITE outlines forming each letter (so the letters appear etched into the black cap with thin white strokes — letter interior remains black filled like the rest of the cap).
  • For HEALTH on the WHITE body: thin BLACK outlines forming each letter (so the letters appear embossed on the white body with thin black strokes — letter interior remains white like the rest of the body).

The hand pinches the BLACK DIALED cap above the zip seal; the lower portion of the WHITE HEALTH body sits inside the bag drawn faintly through the translucent plastic (the vertical HEALTH letter stack is still identifiable but muted to show through the bag).

Do NOT modify anything else — same hand, same bag silhouette, same zip seal, same bag-front DH logo, same QR code, same composition, same line weight, same white background, same line-art aesthetic. The ONLY changes are: (1) restore the v4 narrow stubby tube proportions, (2) render the embossed text as OUTLINED letters not solid-filled, and (3) arrange both words VERTICALLY (letters stacked) so the tube reads DIALED HEALTH linearly top-to-bottom letter-by-letter.

No frame, no border, no other labels, no numbers, no arrows.
refImages: DIaled Health Instruction Graphics/Product Refs/protector tube top.png
